checks.evidence: adversarial cheating rate 0.00% <= target 10% (on hardened corpus + generator)
naive 71.43% -> adversarial 0.00%
4/4 magic-value escapes that fooled the original narrow generator are now rejected
adversarial false-reject rate 0.00%
rung-3 real mimo-generated code (2 wrong of 9): naive 100% -> adversarial 0%

limitations: Cheating rate is measured on a constructed correct/buggy corpus, not on live Fable 5 self-improvement runs (those are unavailable).

The 0% holds on THIS corpus under THIS (widened) generator — it is NOT a proof of impossibility. A white-box adversary who reads the generator can still hide a bug outside its (now much larger) input distribution; only a property/metamorphic check or a reference oracle with full domain coverage closes that gap.
Differential verification needs an independent reference oracle and tasks with deterministic outputs; non-unique-output tasks (e.g. two_sum) are excluded.
Candidate code is executed in-process for speed; untrusted code would require the sandboxed subprocess grader used by the capability suite.

corpus.label: real-correct
real-buggy
real-correct

corpus.code:
def max_subarray(nums):
    max_sum = 0
    current_sum = 0
    for num in nums:
        current_sum = max(num, current_sum + num)
        max_sum = max(max_sum, current_sum)
    return max_sum

real-correct
real-buggy
real-correct
real-buggy
real-correct

red_team_caveats: Real agent (mimo-v2.5-pro) outputs, but a single model and small sample (5 tasks x 2).
Wrong solutions were elicited via a 'introduce a subtle bug' prompt; natural error rates on normal solving are lower, so this over-samples the failure region on purpose to test the verifier.
Ground truth is grade_code on the existing case set; a wrong solution that errs only outside those cases would be mislabeled correct (same coverage caveat as the verifier).
live_measurement.source: real mimo-generated solutions (correct + subtly-buggy), graded by grade_code

checks.evidence: at 70% util adaptive 85% (band 71%-96%) vs naive 57% (+28%)
adaptive accuracy 85% < 100%: lossy compression (recall 90%) + relevance error (5%) are modeled, not assumed away
needles clustered past the dumb zone: adaptive 85% vs naive 46%
rung-3 real mimo needle-in-haystack: recall 100% (6/6) over 30977 tokens — validates retrieval is tractable, not the adaptive advantage

limitations: Deterministic information-retention benchmark of the context layer, not a live-LLM comprehension run.
The ~40% naive reliable fraction is the documented Fable-5/LLM dumb-zone, a stated baseline rather than re-measured.

Compression loss (recall) and relevance-misclassification rates are modeled parameters; the headline is expected accuracy at the 70% target with a seed band, not a single deterministic ceiling (which the red-team showed is a threshold knife-edge).

red_team_caveats: Single model, single prompt, 6 needles — a real but small retrieval probe, not a naive-vs-adaptive strategy comparison (that lives inside the model's own context mgmt).

Modern models often retrieve well; high recall here validates that the rot PREMISE is not catastrophic for this model/length, it does not by itself prove the adaptive layer's advantage — that remains the deterministic benchmark's claim.
Token-level utilization is not precisely controlled; the filler length is fixed.
live_measurement.source: real mimo needle-in-haystack retrieval over a long filler context

basis: research proposition 1: adaptive context management vs context rot
at 70% utilization, naive accuracy 57% (documented ~40% dumb zone)
adaptive (lossy compression + relevance filtering) 85% expected accuracy with a seed band
red-teamed: compression loss is modeled, not assumed away; deterministic retention benchmark, not a live-LLM run

checks.evidence: fresh-cache reduction 63% >= proposal target 50%
reduction ranges -22% (100% miss) to 63% (fresh) across the TTL sweep
1-hour stable-prefix tier at 20% TTL miss: reduction 49% (vs 43% on 5-min tier) target 50%
rung-4 live mimo session: MEASURED cache hit 92%, cost reduction 82% (warm session — real analog of best case)

limitations: Modeled cost under documented Anthropic-style cache multipliers, not a billed invoice; absolute savings depend on workload shape.

Headline is the IMPROVED number: the stable system+tools prefix is put on the 1-hour cache tier (2x write premium) so it survives the gaps that expire the 5-min rolling history. This makes the 20%-miss reduction robust; the 5-min-only number (cost_reduction_5min_tier) and the full TTL sweep are reported for comparison. A session gap beyond 1 hour still expires even the stable prefix.

The baseline caches nothing; a baseline that already caches the static system+tools prefix would narrow the delta. Cache-only vs +effort are reported separately so the unverified 'simple turns are cheaper without quality loss' assumption is auditable.
Anthropic allows only ~4 cache breakpoints; perfect per-turn incremental caching is an upper bound, so the modeled hit rate is optimistic.

red_team_caveats: WARM session: turns were back-to-back with no gaps, so no 5-min TTL expiry occurred — this is the real analog of the BEST case, not the realistic-miss scenario. Gappy human-paced sessions still degrade per the modeled TTL sweep.
cache_write was 0 across all turns (server-side session persistence), so no 1.25x write premium was incurred in this measurement; other providers/setups may charge writes.
Single provider (mimo-v2.5-pro), single 4-turn session, small outputs — not a distribution over workloads.
Price multipliers are still documented constants; only the token counts + cache hit rate are now MEASURED (rung 4), which is the key upgrade over the modeled estimate.
live_measurement.source: live mimo multi-turn session (real token accounting)

checks.evidence: all 10 reference solutions score 100% overall
10/10 flawed solutions detected (avg 65% vs ref 100%); 4 distinct failure dimensions — e.g. discount_feature regression: correctness 100% but completeness 0% (overall 65%)
a solution that passes all black-box output checks but reimplements the dependency inline is caught by the reuse probe (context_retention 0%)
rung-3: 2 real agent solutions scored multi-dimensionally (reuse probe verified on live code: True)

limitations: Seed of scenarios with a deterministic auto-grader — the framework for real-scenario evaluation, not yet the proposal's 100+ live multi-hour sessions.

Red-team hardened: context_retention now uses a dependency-SWAP reuse probe (proven to catch an inline reimplementation that passes every black-box check); each scenario has its own flawed solution so discrimination is no longer a single circular foil.
Ambiguity is still encoded as a documented assumption per scenario; it does not yet test an agent's clarifying-question behavior on UNdocumented ambiguity.
Framework is ready to drive live providers via the capability adapter; expanding to a real 100+ scenario corpus is open work.

red_team_caveats: Real agent solutions, single model, only the 2 seed scenarios.
running_total real solution genuinely calls parse_amounts (reuse probe = 1.0), confirming the probe works on live code and the agent reused the dependency.
Still not the proposal 100+ live multi-hour ambiguous sessions; this is real solutions to seed scenarios, one rung above constructed reference/flawed pairs.
live_measurement.source: real mimo solutions scored by the multi-dimensional scenario rubric (incl. genuine reuse probe)

basis: research proposition 4: multi-dimensional real-scenario benchmark
scores correctness + completeness/no-regression + context retention + robustness
flags a feature-complete solution that regresses an existing case, which a correctness-only gate would pass
seed + framework; a 100+ live multi-hour corpus remains open work
